text: Tell me everything you can remember about the lost wallet — when you created it, what you did with it, any details that come to mind. Do not edit yourself; even seemingly trivial details help.
What is the very first thing that comes to mind when you picture the moment you set up this wallet?
What is the most recent moment you knew the seed phrase or password was still accessible to you?
Where were you physically when you created or last used this wallet? Describe the room, lighting, sounds, anything around you.
What was happening in your life that week — work, travel, anything stressful or memorable?
What device did you create the wallet on, and what state was the device in (new, old, lent from a friend)?
Were you logged into any other accounts at the same time — email, password manager, exchange? Which ones?
Walk me from now backwards: the last time you tried to access this wallet, before that, before that. What made each attempt fail?
In reverse, from most recent: where might you have written down or stored anything related to this wallet?
Imagine watching yourself from above the day you set up this wallet. What does past-you do first, second, third?
If a close friend who knew you well at that time were here, what would they remember about this wallet that you might not?
What kind of wallet is it — Bitcoin Core, Electrum, MetaMask, Phantom, Ledger, Trezor, exchange account, paper wallet? Be specific.
Which blockchain network and address format? (Bitcoin segwit, Ethereum 0x, Solana base58, etc.)
Did this wallet use a 12-word, 24-word, or longer seed phrase? Or no seed at all (e.g. brain wallet, exchange password)?
Was there an additional passphrase or 'hidden wallet' feature enabled (BIP-39 passphrase, Trezor passphrase, Ledger passphrase)?
When was this wallet created — exact date if possible, otherwise the year and season?
What kinds of passwords did you typically use during that period of your life? Common roots, numbers, special characters?
Do you have a password manager from that era? What patterns appear across the entries?
Was the passphrase a meaningful sentence or phrase to you? A song lyric, book quote, inside joke?
Did you use any specific number sequences in passwords during that period (birthdays, anniversaries, addresses)?
Were there any rules you imposed on yourself — minimum length, must contain special character, must not be a real word?
Did you write the seed or password on paper? Where might that paper be — a safe, a drawer, inside a book, taped to a wall?
Did you use a metal seed plate, engraving, or similar physical backup?
Could the seed have been on a sticker, label, postcard, t-shirt, tattoo, or any unconventional medium?
Did you use a hardware wallet? If yes, do you still have the physical device, and do you remember the PIN?
Where do you keep important paper documents from that era today (lease, taxes, medical records)? The seed might be filed alongside.
Do you have any backups from that period — Time Machine, iCloud, Google Drive, Dropbox, an old hard drive in a box?
Did you ever take a photo of the seed phrase, even briefly, with any device?
Did you store the seed or password in any password manager (1Password, KeePass, LastPass, Apple Keychain, browser autofill)?
Do you have email backups (Gmail Takeout, IMAP archive)? You may have emailed yourself a hint or instructions.
Did you use any cloud notes app (Apple Notes, Google Keep, Notion, Obsidian) during that period? An old note may contain a fragment.
Did you ever paste the seed or password into a chat app (Telegram saved messages, WhatsApp self-chat, Signal note to self)?
Do you use a clipboard manager? It may have logged the seed when you copied it once.
Have you searched your old browser history and downloaded files from that era? Any wallet-creation pages or PDFs?
Did this wallet ever hold significant funds, or was it small? The amount affects how careful you might have been with backups.
Was anyone with you when you set this up — partner, friend, family member, coworker?
What feeling do you associate with this wallet now — regret, hope, frustration? Sometimes emotional context aids recall.
If you were teaching someone else how you set up this kind of wallet, walk through the steps as a tutorial. What do you say?
Working backward through your devices: what computers and phones have you owned since this wallet was created? Any still accessible?
When you choose words for passphrases, do you tend toward English, your native language, mixed, made-up?
Have you moved house since you created this wallet? Could the seed be in a box of items you have not unpacked?
Could the seed be in a draft of an email or document you never sent or finalized?
Did the wallet have a custom derivation path or non-standard configuration? (More common with multi-account hardware setups.)
Was the wallet a gift from someone, or did you set it up specifically because someone recommended you do so?
Anything else — even something that seems irrelevant or embarrassing — that you remember from around the time of this wallet?
What is the public address of the wallet, if you know it? We can verify what is on-chain and narrow recovery scope.
If you used a passphrase, did it have a specific theme — a hobby, a person you cared about, a place?
Did you split the seed (e.g. SLIP-39, Shamir, or just half-and-half across two locations)?
Have you searched your spouse's, partner's, or close family member's devices? They may have helped or have a copy.
Roughly how much have you searched for the seed already? Hours, days, weeks? What have you tried that did not work?
If the wallet were to magically open right now, what would you do with the funds? (This is just to surface motivation; it has no bearing on recovery.)
